eventId.description: Event the user is now a planning member of.

reason.description: Present when outcome is invalid or skipped_existing (duplicate in batch).

amountMinor.description: Price in kobo (NGN minor unit) as a string — BigInt-safe.

featurePatch.description: Patch deep-merged onto event.features when this add-on is purchased.

invitees.description: Up to 500 invitees in one go. Server enforces tier max.

cancelled.description: How many scheduled reminders were transitioned to cancelled.

costCents.description: Cost of *this* call in USD cents (Opus is ~5x Sonnet so expect 10-30¢ a draft).

description.example: Surprise 30th for Bola — ~40 guests, Lekki, modest budget, last Saturday of July.

scheduledDate.description: YYYY-MM-DD or null when the user was vague.

description.example: Outdoor or rooftop venue in Lekki with capacity for 40, ideally one Bola has never been to.

scheduledDate.description: Provisional event date; can change while planning.

reason.description: Optional reason for objecting. Strongly encouraged for productive deliberation.

addonSlugs.description: Optional add-on slugs purchased alongside the tier.

callbackUrl.description: Where Paystack redirects after hosted-checkout completes. Defaults to WEB_ORIGIN/events/:id/return.

notes.description: Optional context — reasoning, links, cost estimate, capacity notes.

summary.description: Short summary of the proposed answer (e.g. "Lola's Rooftop, Yaba").

position.description: Optional explicit position. Defaults to the end of the list.

segmentType.description: Type slug — drives the card icon. Use one of the known set (date_time, venue, budget, agenda, guest_list, roles, theme, travel) for a meaningful icon, or anything else for the generic fallback.

profile.description: EPDEMA-side profile row. Auto-created on first /users/me call.

description.description: Free-form description of the event the user wants to plan. The copilot reads this and proposes title + type + date + initial segments.
description.example: 30th birthday for my partner Bola, surprise-style, ~40 close friends, modest budget, somewhere in Lekki, last Saturday of July.

reason.example: Capacity is too tight if we keep the plus-ones list as is.

payload.description: Shape depends on the parent segment.segmentType — validated server-side per type. E.g. a venue proposal has { name, address, capacity }; a date_time proposal has { date, startsAt }.

proposedBy.description: Null when the proposal is anonymous (event has the 'anonymous_proposals' add-on enabled).

currentUserRole.description: The viewing user's role on this event. Populated on single-event reads (GET /:id) and after mutations; absent on list responses.

features.description: Tier-derived feature bag. Shape: { sharing: {...}, branding: {...}, invitees: {...}, … }.

scheduledDate.description: YYYY-MM-DD; null when no date is locked yet.

comment.description: Free-form comment. Surfaced only when the viewer is the reviewee or has admin rights on the event — null otherwise.

isPrivileged.description: Whether the viewer can see all reviews (admin/creator) vs only their own row. Lets the UI decide whether to render the full list or just the "you reviewed" card.

comment.description: Free-form comment. Surfaced only when the viewer is the reviewee or has admin rights on the event — null otherwise.

reviewer.description: Reviewer profile snippet — null for non-privileged viewers.

status.description: Overall status — "degraded" when any dependency check fails.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the check was performed.

costCents.description: Cost of this call in USD cents. 0 on `latest()` reads.

scannedDate.description: The scheduledDate the scan was run against. The one-shot enforcement uses this so a date change unlocks a re-scan.

detail.example: June is peak rainy season — outdoor venues at risk. Consider a covered backup or shift to October.

severity.description: info = worth-knowing context; warning = likely friction; critical = certain conflict.

customAnswers.description: Answers to custom RSVP questions, keyed by question id.

invitedAt.description: When the invitation email was sent; null if not yet invited.

respondedAt.description: When the invitee submitted their RSVP; null if still pending.

rsvpToken.description: Unguessable base64url token (~32 chars) — admin can reshare out-of-band if email failed.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp the recipient accepted; null while pending/declined/revoked/expired.

acceptUrl.description: Full URL the recipient can use to accept. Same token is in the email; admins copy this to share manually.

emailError.description: Error message if email delivery failed; absent on success.
emailError.example: Resend sandbox: recipient not on allowlist

emailSent.description: Whether the invitation email was sent successfully (best-effort).

inviterId.description: User id of the admin who sent the invitation.

proposalId.description: The proposal that wins. Must belong to this segment and be live with zero live objections.

reason.example: Capacity is too tight if we keep the plus-ones list as is.

amountMinor.description: Amount in kobo (NGN minor unit) as a string — BigInt-safe.

processorReference.description: Paystack reference (UUID) — null for the free-tier internal processor.

purposeRef.description: Slug of the tier or add-on this payment unlocks.

taxMinor.description: Nigerian VAT in kobo as a string — BigInt-safe.

amountMinor.description: Total amount in kobo as a string — BigInt-safe.

authorizationUrl.description: Paystack-hosted checkout URL. Null for free-tier (auto-success) or replays — the client should poll payment status instead of redirecting.

reference.description: Paystack reference — null for the free-tier internal processor.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp the recipient accepted; null while pending/declined/revoked/expired.

inviterId.description: User id of the admin who sent the invitation.

invitedById.description: The member who invited this user. Null for the event creator (auto-added as first admin).

reason.example: Capacity is too tight if we keep the plus-ones list as is.

payload.description: Shape depends on the parent segment.segmentType — validated server-side per type. E.g. a venue proposal has { name, address, capacity }; a date_time proposal has { date, startsAt }.

proposedBy.description: Null when the proposal is anonymous (event has the 'anonymous_proposals' add-on enabled).

ratingAvg.description: Decimal-as-string. `null` when the creator opted out of public rating OR has zero reviews. Use together with `ratingCount`.

ratingCount.description: Always paired with `ratingAvg`. `0` here = "hide the badge entirely" on the public page.

value.description: The locked decision payload. Shape varies by segmentType (venue, time, menu, …).

scheduledDate.description: YYYY-MM-DD; null when no date is locked yet.

brand.description: Public brand payload — null when the event's tier doesn't grant the customSharePage feature, otherwise the organizer's configured colors/logo/cover.

scheduledDate.description: YYYY-MM-DD; null when no date is locked yet.

brand.description: Custom brand for the share page. Null when the event tier does not grant custom branding, or when the organizer has not configured anything.

scheduledDate.description: YYYY-MM-DD; null when no date is locked yet.

details.description: Free-form details about the blocker (e.g. which segment is unlocked).

customSlug.description: Reserved — custom share slug requires Occasion+ tier (Phase 5c).

slug.description: New share slug. Lowercase letters, digits, and single hyphens, 3-64 chars. Reserved words (api, admin, etc.) are rejected.

orderedIds.description: Segment IDs in their new order. Must list every segment of the event exactly once.

comment.description: Free-form comment. Surfaced only when the viewer is the reviewee or has admin rights on the event — null otherwise.
Free-form comment. Surfaced only when the viewer is the reviewee or has admin rights on the event — null otherwise.

options.description: Present when type=select; the choices an invitee can pick.

inviteeId.description: Null for event-wide reminders (e.g. event_tomorrow broadcasts).

sentAt.description: When the reminder actually fired; null until status=sent.

description.example: Outdoor garden venue within 30 minutes of the church, capacity 120+.

lockedValue.description: Final decided payload once the segment is locked. Shape varies by segmentType (e.g. a venue payload has { name, address, capacity }).

segmentType.description: Free-form short slug ('date_time' | 'venue' | 'budget' | 'agenda' | 'guest_list' | 'roles' | 'theme' | 'travel' | …). Drives the picker icon on the planning board.

description.example: Outdoor garden venue within 30 minutes of the church, capacity 120+.

lockedValue.description: Final decided payload once the segment is locked. Shape varies by segmentType (e.g. a venue payload has { name, address, capacity }).

segmentType.description: Free-form short slug ('date_time' | 'venue' | 'budget' | 'agenda' | 'guest_list' | 'roles' | 'theme' | 'travel' | …). Drives the picker icon on the planning board.

notes.example: Outdoor garden with shaded marquee, capacity ~180. Public path nearby — lock 3-4pm Saturday to keep traffic light.

reason.example: Matches your 24 Jun date (dry-season weekend) and the outdoor steer.

summary.example: Garden venue at Lekki Conservation Centre

typed.description: Optional typed payload for segments where structured data fits.

costCents.description: Cost of *this* call in USD cents. Surface a tiny '≈X¢' badge so the admin can see what each suggestion run spent.

error.description: Present when sent=false; the underlying delivery error message.

questions.description: Full replacement of the question set. Pass [] to remove all questions. Server validates 'select' questions have ≥1 option and tier cap (Occasion=3, Production=10, Marquee=∞).

comment.description: Optional free-form comment. Only visible to the event creator (visibility rule per Phase 7 plan — public surface shows the aggregate only).
comment.example: Tunde organised every segment crisply — the proposal-objection rhythm meant nobody felt steamrolled.

customAnswers.description: Reserved for Phase 5c custom RSVP questions.

name.description: Optional name correction — used when an invitee was added by email only.

steer.description: Optional free-form steer for the suggestions — e.g. "focus on Lagos vendors", "outdoor only", "stay under ₦300k".

amountMinor.description: Price in kobo (NGN minor unit) as a string — amounts are BigInt server-side and JSON-serialized as strings so values above 2^53 stay precise.

featureTemplate.description: Default feature bag copied onto event.features at provisioning. Shape: { sharing: {...}, branding: {...}, invitees: {...}, … }.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for unlocking. Strongly encouraged for audit clarity.

accentColor.description: Accent color for highlights, links, RSVP CTA.

color.description: Primary brand color. Hex with leading #.

textColor.description: Text color for content sitting on top of the brand color.

scheduledDate.description: Provisional event date; can change while planning.

ratingVisible.description: When false, public surfaces (share page) omit your rating entirely. Default true — opt-out, not opt-in, since the rating is the trust signal.

ratingAvg.description: Average rating as a decimal string (Prisma Decimal serializes to string).

brandApplied.description: Whether event.brand color slots were also updated. True except in edge cases (e.g. parsing failed mid-write).

costCents.description: Cost of this call in USD cents.

example: String lights overhead in concentric loops
Long shared table with mismatched candles

palette.description: Exactly 3 hex colors: [dominant, accent, text-on-dominant]. Auto-written to event.brand.

info.description: Event Planning Decision Making Assistant. Spec frozen per Phase 5.5 — bump `info.version` (major for breaking, minor for additive) on every committed change.
info.title: EPDEMA API

200.description: List active à-la-carte add-ons.

AI-generated event scaffold: { title, eventType, scheduledDate?, description?, segments: [{ segmentType, title, description }], costCents }. The web wizard pre-fills its form from this; the user can edit anything before final create.

post.summary: Draft an event scaffold from a free-form description (Production+ Opus)

200.description: Events created by the authenticated user or where they are a planning member.
Create a draft event. No payment yet — tier is chosen later.
Read the event's brand (admin only). Returns null fields when nothing has been configured yet — clients should fall back to default chrome.
Update brand colors (admin only, Production+ tier gated). Pass each color as a hex string with leading "#". Omit a field to leave it unchanged; pass null to clear it.
Remove the cover image (admin).
Upload a cover image (admin, Production+). PNG / JPEG / WebP, max 6 MB.
Remove the logo (admin).
Upload a logo (admin, Production+). PNG / JPEG / WebP / SVG, max 2 MB. The previous logo is deleted from storage after the new one persists.

Returns the most recent persisted scan (warnings + scannedDate) so the event detail page can surface them without re-rolling Claude. When nothing's been scanned yet, returns `{ warnings: [], scannedDate: '', costCents: 0 }`.

get.summary: Read the most recent holiday-scan result (no new model call)

200.description: Generates a fresh scan against the current event.scheduledDate. Returns 403 when the event doesn't have the `holiday_scanner` add-on; 409 when a scan already exists for this date (re-run after changing the date, or buy the add-on again to re-roll).

post.summary: Run the holiday & conflict scan for an event (admin, add-on required)

200.description: List all invitees for the event (admin only).
Add a single invitee by email. Returns a one-item result array so the admin sees the same shape as the bulk endpoint.
Remove an invitee (admin only).
Add up to 500 invitees in one go. Returns a per-row result list ('created' | 'updated' | 'skipped_existing' | 'invalid') so the admin can see exactly what happened to each entry.
RSVP status breakdown — { pending, yes, no, maybe, total }.
Read the event's custom RSVP question set (admin only).

get.summary: Read the event's custom RSVP question set

200.description: Replace the event's custom RSVP question set (admin only, Occasion+ gated, tier cap enforced). Pass [] to remove all questions.

put.summary: Replace the event's custom RSVP question set

200.description: List scheduled + recent reminders for this event (admin only). Used by the admin invitees page to show 'N reminders scheduled' with status.
Cancel every still-scheduled reminder for this event (admin only). History rows stay visible with status="cancelled".
Send the "you're invited" email to invitees. By default only those who have not yet been emailed; pass ?all=true to re-send to everyone.
List planning members of the event.

get.summary: List planning members of an event

200.description: Remove a member from the event (admin only). No body.
Change a member's role (admin only).
List pending invitations for the event (admin only).
Invite an email to the event (admin only). Response includes acceptUrl so admins can share manually when email delivery is unreliable.

post.summary: Invite an email to the planning team (admin)

200.description: Revoke a pending invitation (admin only). No body.
Admin or creator gets every review with reviewer + comment hydrated. A non-privileged planning member sees only their own row. Non-members get 403 from the assertMember guard.

get.summary: List reviews on this event (admin/creator see all; member sees own)

200.description: Submit one review per planning member per event. Event must be in state `past`. Reviewer cannot review their own event. 409 on a second submission for the same (reviewer, event). Atomic with the rolling average update on the creator's `user_profile.ratingAvg`.

post.summary: Submit a 1-5 review of the event creator (planning member only)

200.description: List segments for an event (ordered by position).

201.description: Add a custom segment (admin only). Respects tier maxSegments cap.

200.description: Delete a segment (admin only). Refused if the segment is locked or has live proposals. Returns 204 No Content on success.
Segment detail with live proposals + objections embedded. One call for the segment-detail page.
Rename or edit a segment (admin only). Works on locked segments too.
Lock the segment with the chosen proposal as the winner. Atomic state transition under serializable txn + per-segment advisory lock.

post.summary: Lock a segment with a winning proposal

200.description: Returns 3-5 AI-generated proposal candidates. Each carries a `reason` explaining why it fits THIS event. Admin clicks one to instantiate as a real Proposal via the existing POST /proposals endpoint. Tier-gated on `features.ai.perSegmentSuggestions` (Occasion+); 403 below that.

post.summary: Suggest 3-5 proposal candidates for a segment (admin, Occasion+)

200.description: Unlock a locked segment. Restores all proposals to live state; clears lockedValue.
Reorder all segments in one atomic transaction (admin only). Must list every segment ID exactly once.

Generates the one-shot vibe pack: theme name, palette, decoration + music ideas, emoji. Auto-applies the palette to event.brand. 403 when the event doesn't have the `ai_vibe_pack` add-on; 409 when already generated.

post.summary: Generate the vibe pack for an event (admin, add-on required)

200.description: Get an event the authenticated user is a planning member of (or owns).
Update a draft event (title, type, date, description).

Phase 7·A — admin-only manual transition from published → past. Required before planning members can submit reviews. Idempotent on already-past events. 400 when state is not published, or when scheduledDate is null/future. In 7·D, a worker will fire this automatically once the scheduled date passes.

post.summary: Flip a published event to past state (admin)

200.description: Publish the event (admin only). Validates all blockers; generates a share slug; broadcasts event.changed.
Which blockers remain (unlocked segments, missing date, odd voting count). Used by the pre-publish modal.
Rename the public share slug (admin only, Occasion-tier feature). Format: lowercase letters/digits/single hyphens, 3-64 chars. Reserved words and taken slugs return 409.
Roll back to planning state (admin only). Preserves the share slug so re-publishing keeps the same URL.
Liveness + dependency check (Postgres + Redis).

parameters.description: Opaque invitation token (64-char hex) emailed to the recipient and returned in acceptUrl.
parameters.name: token

200.description: Public peek at an invitation. Returns event title, inviter, expiry — no sensitive data.

get.summary: Public peek at a planning invitation (unauthenticated)

parameters.description: Opaque invitation token (64-char hex) emailed to the recipient and returned in acceptUrl.
parameters.name: token

200.description: Accept an invitation. Requires signed-in user whose email matches.
Withdraw an objection (objector only).

parameters.description: When "true" or "1", calls Paystack verify if the payment is still pending. Use on the return page.
parameters.name: reconcile

200.description: Get a payment owned by the authenticated user.

parameters.description: Paystack reference echoed back on the return-page redirect.
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: When "true" or "1", calls Paystack verify if the payment is still pending. Use on the return page.
parameters.name: reconcile

200.description: Look up a payment by Paystack reference (used by the return page since Paystack only echoes `reference` in the redirect).

get.summary: Look up a payment by Paystack reference

201.description: Created (or replayed) a payment intent. Returns a Paystack checkout URL for paid tiers; free tier short-circuits to status=success with no URL.

200.description: List live objections against a proposal.

get.summary: List live objections on a proposal

201.description: Raise an objection against a proposal.

post.summary: Raise an objection on a proposal

200.description: Withdraw a proposal (proposer only).

parameters.description: RSVP token from the invitation URL — base64url of 24 random bytes (~32 chars).
parameters.name: token

200.description: Look up an invitation by RSVP token. Returns event essentials, the invitee's own status, and (Production+ tier) the event brand so the RSVP page can render in the organizer's theme.

get.summary: Look up an invitation by token

parameters.description: RSVP token from the invitation URL — base64url of 24 random bytes (~32 chars).
parameters.name: token

200.description: Submit / update an RSVP (yes / no / maybe). Idempotent — the same invitee can change their mind any time before the event.
List live proposals for a segment.

get.summary: List live proposals on a segment

201.description: Create a proposal on a segment.

post.summary: Create a proposal on a segment

parameters.description: Public share slug (lowercase letters/digits/single hyphens, 3-64 chars).
parameters.name: slug

200.description: Public event details — title, date, decided segments, creator name, and (Production+ tier) the event brand. No PII.

get.summary: Get the public share page for an event

parameters.description: Public share slug (lowercase letters/digits/single hyphens, 3-64 chars).
parameters.name: slug

200.description: iCalendar (RFC 5545) file with a single VEVENT for the scheduled date. Returns 404 (text/plain) when the event has no scheduled date yet.

get.summary: Download the event as an .ics calendar file

200.description: List active tiers (Free → Marquee) with prices in kobo.
Authenticated user + EPDEMA profile (auto-created on first call).

patch.description:
Phase 7·F — partial update for the authenticated user's profile. Used
by the settings UI to toggle `ratingVisible` (the headline gating for
public rating display) and edit display fields. Upsert semantics:
absent fields are not modified.

200.description: Returns the updated profile. Setting `ratingVisible=false` hides the creator’s rating from the public share page immediately — no cache invalidation needed, the share serializer re-reads on every request.

get.summary: List reviews of my events (creator surface; comments included for self)
